<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("=== CURRENT STATE ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] <= 20:
        boundary = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in boundary]
        cols = [p[1] for p in boundary]
        print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, rows[{min(rows)}-{max(rows)}], cols[{min(cols)}-{max(cols)}], children={n['children']}")

ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nLeft panel area:")
for i in range(32, 37):
    print(f"Row {i}: {ascii_lines[i][8:26]}")

print("\nGap area:")
for i in range(28, 35):
    print(f"Row {i}: {ascii_lines[i][26:42]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK so the left agent moved from cols 18-20 to cols 21-23. The c-frame is still intact with the white square inside. So pressing RIGHT when the active agent is blocked continues to move the OTHER agent (the left one) to the right.
Pattern so far:
- Right agent active, blocked by purple bar
- Press RIGHT → left agent moves right by 3 cells
- 1st RIGHT: left agent 12-14 → 15-17 (kicked out of c-frame, wall breaks)
- 2nd RIGHT: left agent 15-17 → 18-20 (c-frame restores)
- 3rd RIGHT: left agent 18-20 → 21-23 (c-frame stays intact)
So the left agent keeps moving right each time I press RIGHT. It's now at cols 21-23, approaching the gap area where the right agent is.
Let me think about what happens if the left agent keeps moving right and reaches the right agent's position. Maybe they collide or swap or something.
But actually, I've been wasting a lot of actions. I'm at step 25 with about 28 target actions. I need to figure out the goal quickly.
Let me think about what I know:
1. Two agents (N border), two c-frames (c border)
2. Each c-frame has a white 3x3 square inside
3. When an agent enters a c-frame, it replaces the white square and its center becomes c
4. Clicking on an agent activates it (and swaps center colors between agents)
5. When the active agent is blocked, pressing a direction moves the OTHER agent
6. The left agent can enter the left c-frame, changing its center to c
7. The right agent can't reach the right c-frame due to the purple bar
Maybe the goal is to get BOTH agents into their respective c-frames. Since the right agent can't reach the right c-frame directly, maybe I need to:
1. Get the right agent's center to c (done by swapping)
2. Use the "blocked push" mechanic to move the left agent out of the way
3. Then somehow get the right agent into the right c-frame
But the right agent is still blocked by the purple bar...
Wait, maybe the goal is different. Maybe I need to move the left agent all the way to the right panel, into the right c-frame. Let me keep pressing RIGHT to see what happens when the left agent reaches the right agent's position or the purple bar.
Actually, let me first check: can the left agent pass through the right agent? And can it pass through the purple bar?
Let me press RIGHT a few more times to move the left agent closer to the purple bar.

<parameter=code>
# Keep pressing RIGHT to move the left agent toward the purple bar
# Left agent is at cols 21-23, right agent at 30-32, purple bar at 33-38
# Need to move left agent to col ~27-29 to be adjacent to right agent
# Each RIGHT moves it 3 cols, so 1-2 more RIGHTs should get it there
action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
